question_frequency.rationale: The Tutor asks essentially no didactic questions. It dumps a complete solution upfront and then responds to the student's questions rather than driving inquiry. No teachable-moment prompts surfaced.

question_quality.rationale: Since there are effectively no Tutor-posed questions or forks, there's nothing substantive to evaluate. The student drove the only conceptual probe (the +1 question).

scaffolding.rationale: The full solution including code, complexity, and trace is revealed in turn 1. There's no progressive build-up — every spoiler is given immediately, so no scaffolding exists.

flow_and_tone.rationale: Tone is warm and explanations are clear and accurate, with good follow-up on the +1 nuance and edge cases. But it's lecture-then-confirm rather than a true dialogue.

effectiveness.rationale: Final code is correct and equivalent to baseline (only minor difference: returns -1 instead of raising on the unreachable branch, which the Tutor flagged).

friction.rationale: Two turns, ~75s, comparable to baseline's 54s. Slightly longer but with marginal extra discussion of the +1 bias and edge cases; no significant friction.
